probes.name: run_full_priority_review
probes.prompt: Hey, can you run a full priority review on all my current tasks and goals? I feel like I've been just reacting to stuff and I want to see what actually matters right now and get a strategy for the week.
probes.criteria:
The priority_orchestrator agent must acknowledge the request to run the full priority review chain (priority_auditor → strategy_creator → strategy_analyzer → compile-and-report). It should NOT run tools itself but explain it will delegate through the ordered chain and compile a final report. A good response outlines the steps it will orchestrate and returns a compiled summary of priorities with a suggested strategy.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages 15, 17, 24 — user asking to check/review tasks and get grouped/structured view, combined with message 23's desire for planning and structure.

probes.name: overdue_tasks_reprioritize_pl
probes.prompt: Mam dużo zaległych zadań, niektóre to rzeczy z tygodnie temu. Możesz puścić audyt priorytetów i dać mi nową strategię co ogarnąć najpierw? Nie chcę się bałaganić, po prostu mi powiedz co jest ważne a co mogę odpuścić albo przesunąć.
probes.criteria:
The priority_orchestrator must recognize this as a trigger for the full priority review chain. It should delegate to priority_auditor to assess overdue tasks, then strategy_creator to build a plan, then strategy_analyzer to evaluate, and finally compile a report. A good response delivers a structured Polish-language summary: what is truly important, what can be dropped/rescheduled, and a concrete ordering. It must NOT attempt to run individual tools itself.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages 6, 25, 27 — user notices stale/overdue tasks and wants clarity on what to focus on. Polish phrasing matches messages 10, 33.

probes.name: quick_priority_check_morning
probes.prompt: Good morning, can you quickly check my priorities for today? Run the review and tell me what I should focus on first, second, third. I have a hackaton coming up, a CV update deadline this weekend, and some admin tasks like sending an invoice and responding to a job offer from Ania Tabor. I need a clear ordering.
probes.criteria:
The priority_orchestrator must run the priority chain and return a compiled ordered list for the day. A good response factors in the provided context (hackaton, CV deadline, admin tasks) and produces a concrete ranked recommendation with reasoning. It should not just list tasks but actively prioritize based on urgency and importance, reflecting the strategy_creator and strategy_analyzer stages of the chain.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages 9, 21, 23, 29, 38 — morning routine with multiple competing deadlines (CV, hackaton, job offer, invoice).

probes.name: reassess_strategy_effectiveness
probes.prompt: I feel like the priorities from last week didn't really work out — I kept skipping stuff that was supposedly high priority. Can you re-run the full priority review and also analyze what went wrong with the last strategy? I want to understand if my priorities were wrong or if the plan was unrealistic.
probes.criteria:
The priority_orchestrator must delegate through the full chain, with special emphasis on the strategy_analyzer stage to evaluate effectiveness of a previous strategy. A good response provides both a new prioritized plan AND a reflective analysis of what went wrong (e.g., too many high-priority items, unrealistic time estimates, wrong importance scoring). It should compile both the audit findings and strategic recommendations into a cohesive report.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages 27, 32, 19 — user expressing frustration that existing tasks/priorities don't match reality and wanting reassessment.

probes.name: priority_cleanup_stale_goals
probes.prompt: Hej, widzę że mam sporo starych celów i zadań które już nie są aktualne — np. rzeczy związane z obroną pracy, jakieś testowe goal, no i parę rzeczy które już zrobiłem ale nie są oznaczone. Puść full review i daj mi raport co mogę usunąć, co jest martwe, i co faktycznie powinno zostać jako aktywny priorytet.
probes.criteria:
The priority_orchestrator must run the full priority chain with emphasis on the auditor stage to identify stale, completed-but-unmarked, and irrelevant goals. A good response delivers a compiled cleanup report in Polish: items to delete (with reasoning), items already done that should be closed, and items that genuinely remain active priorities. It should not run individual delete/mark operations itself but present findings and recommendations.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages 6, 14, 19, 27, 34 — user identifying dead/stale tasks, requesting cleanup, and wanting clarity on what's actually active. Mixed Polish/English phrasing per messages 2, 10.
